text: Define a canonical benchmark fixture — 90-day window × 50 symbols × 5m interval, drilldown OFF, ~hourly scheduled scans (~2160 scans) — and require its warm-cache end-to-end wall-time to complete in < 10 s (stretch < 5 s).
rationale: Findings size this exact workload at ~1.3M rows / 50–100MB that fits RAM; a concrete, committed canonical case is the unit every phase target is measured against and replaces the vague 'hours→minutes' goal with a testable number.

text: The canonical 90d×50sym run with drilldown ON (Phase B 1m) must complete warm in < 20 s end-to-end.
rationale: drilldown_enabled defaults True (the real default critical path, RC-7/RC-8); a separate budget is needed because the 2-phase path doubles engine passes and adds 1m fetches.

text: A warm identical rerun (same config, same process session) must complete in < 2 s, issue exactly 0 Bybit HTTP calls, and execute 0 kline-table SELECTs (served entirely from the process-level LRU / sealed manifest).
rationale: Directly tests the headline 're-downloads every rerun' fix (RC-3/RC-4/RC-5); the process LRU (§3.2 step 7) must make an unchanged rerun do zero exchange and zero Postgres kline work.

text: Every benchmarked canonical run must finish at ≤ 50% of the 120 s hard cap (≤ 60 s) on the optimized engine; the 120s _TIMEOUT_SECONDS stays a safety net, never a target the run approaches.
rationale: The 120s cap is both symptom and constraint (discovery §4); preserving ≥2× headroom prevents marginal regressions from re-introducing timeouts on slightly larger configs.

text: A cold run (empty cache) must bound Bybit kline requests to ≤ ceil(true_missing_candles / 1000) and must never re-fetch a day already sealed; total cold wall-time is network-bound, not history-bound.
rationale: Fixes RC-4 span-collapse (whole-history re-download) and RC-3 perpetual-gap; with limit=1000 (RC bump) and per-gap-run fetch, cold cost scales with genuinely-missing candles only.

text: Per-scan window setup cost must scale with window size W (candles in [start_time,end_time]), not N_total: a test that 4× the pre-window history at fixed W leaves per-scan setup time constant within ±10%.
rationale: RC-1: _evaluate_candles_until uses continue (not break) on both bounds, touching all N_total candles per scan; searchsorted/slice makes it O(log N + W). This is the single biggest, parity-neutral engine win.

text: Mark-seeding must be sub-quadratic in backtest length: doubling T (and proportional scans S) increases total seeding time ≤ ~2.2× (linear), never ~4× (quadratic), verified by a length-doubling scaling test.
rationale: RC-2: linear prefix-scan-from-index-0 with monotonically advancing start_time is O(P×T²) over the run; merge-walk pointers / searchsorted collapse it to O(P×T) or better.

text: Position price lookup ('last close ≤ T' / 'first open ≥ T') must be implemented as np.searchsorted (O(log N)) or non-resetting merge-walk pointer (O(1)); a microbenchmark asserts lookup time grows ≤ log(N) as per-symbol N doubles.
rationale: RC-2/RC-12 fix; ascending kline lists make bisect provably correct, and pointers that never reset across scans give amortized O(1).

text: Entry-fill-bar resolution and the price-drift check must share a single searchsorted result per signal (one bisect), not two independent linear scans.
rationale: RC-12: _open_position and _apply_filter_chain currently each linear-scan klines for the same next-bar; sharing the index removes a duplicate O(N) pass per signal.

text: Trailing-profit evaluation must be O(P) per candle, not O(P²): with P concurrent positions the per-candle inner iteration count ≤ c·P, and doubling P increases trailing time ≤ ~2×.
rationale: RC-9: caller loops candles_at_time × all open_positions with continue on non-matches = O(P²); the inverse direct-index pattern already exists 90 lines above in the TP/SL loop.

text: Adaptive-blacklist membership must use an incremental per-symbol win/total counter (O(1) per signal), making its cost independent of accumulated closed-trade count.
rationale: RC-16: _is_adaptive_blacklisted rescans ALL closed_trades per candidate signal = O(signals × closed_trades), growing with backtest history; an incrementally-updated counter removes the growth term.

text: Total engine wall-time must scale ≈ linearly with (total_candles × scans): a fixture that doubles backtest length shows ≤ 2.2× engine time, with no measurable super-linear term.
rationale: Aggregate guard that RC-1+RC-2 are both gone; the pre-fix complexity is O(scans × N_total) with a quadratic seeding term, the achievable target is a single merge-walk.

text: Doubling scan count S at fixed kline data must increase engine wall-time ≤ ~2× (linear in S), proving the O(scans × N_total) per-scan setup term is eliminated and merge-walk pointers do not reset across scans.
rationale: The headline blowup is multiplicative in scans (~2160 hourly scans × 1.3M candles ≈ billions of setup iterations); scan-axis scaling must be tested independently of the length axis.

text: Post-Phase-3 (SoA, still pure Python) the engine must sustain ≥ 100k candle-evaluations/sec single-core on the canonical fixture.
rationale: Phase 3 must hit 'minutes' before any JIT (D5 risk: numba on Python 3.14/numpy 2.4 is optional); a throughput floor makes 'pure-Python SoA is enough' testable.

text: Post-Phase-4 (@njit kernel, warmed) the inner per-candle kernel must sustain ≥ 5M candle-evaluations/sec single-core, excluding first-call compile time.
rationale: numba docs cite 1–2 orders of magnitude and ~300× on a path-dependent backtest; a measured floor on the warmed kernel converts that estimate into a gate.

text: _load_klines must issue exactly 1 Postgres query per interval for all symbols via WHERE symbol = ANY($1) (bucketed in Python), independent of symbol count; assert query_count == 1 for an N-symbol load.
rationale: RC-6: current N+1 serial per-symbol round-trips block the event loop on S RTTs before the engine starts and run inside every sweep load_inputs.

text: A sealed closed day must be fetched from Bybit exactly once ever: with a mocked client across ≥3 reruns, the call count for that day == 1, while the still-forming current day is still refreshed.
rationale: §3.2 seal rule + §7 cache-parity test; kills the 144/288→refetch-forever loop (RC-3) without freezing the live-edge candle.

text: REST page size must be raised 200→1000 so gap-fill request count for a fixed missing span is ~5× lower than the 200-page baseline (assert request count ratio ≈ 5×).
rationale: Bybit-documented 1000 cap; also widens the per-call coverage so spans >3.47 days stop truncating to the most-recent 1000 (RC-4 pagination nuance).

text: Each symbol's kline load must be bounded to [min(signal_time) − lookback, max(signal_time) + max_hold_window]; a symbol trading on 1 day of a 90-day run loads ≤ its bounded span, not all ~25,920 candles.
rationale: RC-17: full-range load per symbol wastes rows transferred + resident memory with no engine-semantic change.

text: The process-level _load_klines LRU must be bounded (explicit max entries or max bytes), return byte-identical klines to a fresh Postgres read, and not grow resident memory across reruns in a session.
rationale: §3.2 step 7 memoizes sealed days only; an unbounded memo would leak across a long sweep — the bound and the byte-identical guarantee are both testable (parity + memory).

text: 1m drill-down must fetch only narrow per-bar windows (grouping adjacent epochs), keeping over-fetch ≤ ~20% of fetched candles (vs ~98% discarded today); assert fetched_1m ≈ needed_bars, not the full min→max span.
rationale: RC-7: _build_fine_klines fetches contiguous min→max and discards ~98%; per-bar windows eliminate the dominant drill waste.

text: 1m drill fetches must run via semaphore-bounded asyncio.gather (concurrency ≤ a configured cap), not serial awaits; for K symbols wall-time ≈ ceil(K/cap)×per-fetch, not K×per-fetch.
rationale: RC-7: current per-symbol serial awaits on the default critical path; bounded gather parallelizes without unbounded connection pressure.

text: An in-process 1m cache keyed by (symbol, bar_open_epoch) must make a warm rerun issue 0 Bybit 1m calls; it stays in-memory only (never store_klines) and is bounded/cleared per session.
rationale: §3.2 step 8: persisting 1m would re-poison the 5m coverage table; the in-memory cache fixes the rerun re-pull (RC-7) while preserving cache-table integrity.

text: Configs with no portfolio-equity close rules must run exactly 1 engine pass (Phase A skipped), measurably halving engine wall-time versus the 2-pass drill path on that config subset.
rationale: RC-8: drill-down runs the full sim twice; exit bars are knowable per-trade from TP/SL without a global equity pass when no basket-level rule exists.

text: Sweep combos must execute concurrently via semaphore-bounded asyncio.gather (portable, functioning on win32 where ProcessPool is unavailable); a K-combo sweep wall-time ≈ ceil(K/concurrency)×combo-time, not K×combo-time.
rationale: RC-13: _execute_sweep awaits each combo serially with a 3-thread executor 2/3 idle; supports_process_pool() is False on win32 so the async-gather fix is the portable win.

text: Sweep concurrency must be configurable and default toward cpu_count (above the _MAX_CONCURRENT=3 single-run slot limit); assert effective parallelism > 3 on a multi-core host.
rationale: The 3-slot cap protects interactive single runs; sweeps are embarrassingly parallel and should scale with cores per Phase 2/6.

text: Snapshot/signals/instrument_info must be shipped to sweep workers exactly once (ProcessPool initializer globals or shared memory), so total IPC/pickling bytes are independent of combo count, not O(combos × snapshot_size).
rationale: RC-14: run_sweep_pooled re-pickles the entire dataset per combo via run_in_executor positional args; shipping once removes O(N×snapshot) copy cost.

text: Sweep result ranks must be stamped in a single set-based UPDATE … FROM (VALUES …); total write statements for K combos == K + O(1), not 4K.
rationale: RC-15: current code writes each result then re-writes every ranked result = 2N writes / 4N statements; keep the first per-combo write for crash-resume.

text: A K-combo sweep on M cores must achieve measured speedup ≥ 0.7 × min(M, K, concurrency) versus the serial baseline on the canonical combo (wall-time ≈ single-combo-time × ceil(K / min(M,concurrency)) + O(1) setup).
rationale: Converts 'sweeps parallelize across cores' into a measurable efficiency floor that accounts for setup and scheduling overhead.

text: Peak process RSS for the canonical 90d×50sym run must be ≤ 1 GB, and resident kline memory must be ≤ 2× the theoretical SoA size (~75 MB → ≤ 150 MB klines).
rationale: Working-set arithmetic (§4.1) says the data is ~50–100MB; a memory ceiling prevents SoA conversion / caches / LRU from ballooning resident footprint and guards sweep fan-out.

text: The kline load path must materialize columnar arrays (numpy/Arrow), eliminating the per-row Python dict + six float() casts; assert no per-candle Python object is allocated during bulk load.
rationale: RC-6/RC-11: get_klines builds a dict with six float() casts per row (CPython-bound materialization of millions of rows) and blocks JIT; columnar conversion done once removes it.

text: Warm sweep reruns must slice klines from the in-process Arrow/Polars hot cache (RAM, zero-copy) with 0 disk and 0 Postgres reads after the first load; cross-process warm reruns use mmap'd Feather V2 (no deserialization).
rationale: §4.1 three-layer cache: first sweep iteration loads from disk, every subsequent rerun is a RAM slice — the mechanism that makes warm sweeps near-instant.

text: numba first-call JIT compile (1–10 s) must be excluded from the 120 s run budget: the kernel is warmed once at import/startup (and cache=True persists compiled code), so no user run pays cold-compile inside the timed path.
rationale: numba's slow first call would otherwise consume the latency budget on the first run of each process; warm-once + cache=True amortizes it (research rules §4.2).

text: With numba/pyarrow/duckdb absent (import-guarded fallback active), the pure-Python SoA engine must still meet the 'minutes' bar (< 60 s warm) on the canonical fixture — JIT/columnar are optimizations, not latency dependencies.
rationale: Discovery §10 D5: bleeding-edge Python 3.14/numpy 2.4 may lack numba wheels; the backend must not regress to hours (or fail to import) when heavy deps are missing.

text: A committed, reproducible benchmark harness (fixed fixture, fixed seed, pre-warmed JIT) must report, per phase: cold wall-time, warm wall-time, engine-only CPU time, peak RSS, Bybit call count, and Postgres query count.
rationale: Phase targets are only enforceable if a single deterministic harness emits the exact metrics each requirement above is asserted against.

text: Each phase PR must include a benchmark delta versus the prior phase on the canonical fixture and fail the gate if end-to-end wall-time regresses > 5% (or peak RSS regresses > 10%).
rationale: Prevents a later phase from silently undoing an earlier speedup; makes 'never trust a phase without its benchmark' a CI gate, not a guideline.

text: After Phase 3, a committed profile artifact (cProfile or py-spy) must confirm that per-scan window setup and mark-seeding are no longer in the top-2 hotspots before Phase 4 (njit) is started, re-targeting the JIT at the new dominant cost.
rationale: Findings §9 explicitly says 'profile after Phase 3 to re-target Phase 4'; the asymptotic wins must be confirmed gone before adding JIT complexity.

text: A scaling/headline test must assert the optimized canonical warm run is ≥ 100× faster end-to-end than the pre-optimization baseline captured and frozen in Phase 0.
rationale: Findings claim ≈100–1000× on long scan-heavy backtests; freezing the Phase-0 baseline lets the headline speedup be a regression-guarded number rather than a marketing estimate.

text: Each run must record a per-stage timing breakdown (warmup band, kline load, engine sim, 1m drill, persist) so any latency-budget regression is attributable to a specific stage.
rationale: The progress pipeline already bands warm-up at 10%; stage-level timing turns a slow run into an actionable diagnosis instead of a single opaque wall-time.

text: The optional Phase-6 vectorized first-touch fast-path must be gated strictly on independent-position configs (max_drawdown_pct ≥ 100, no close_on_profit, no profit_pct target, no trailing_profit, no breakeven mutation) and, when active, deliver ≥ 10× speedup over the sequential kernel while producing identical trades.
rationale: §4.2/§5: the fast-path only applies where positions are provably independent; the speedup must be earned and validated trade-by-trade against the sequential oracle (anything ambiguous routes to the sequential kernel).

text: Intrabar exit precedence MUST remain liquidation → SL → TP with the pessimistic resolution: when both SL and TP touch inside one bar, SL wins; SL wins only if closer than the liquidation price; and the existing `>0` guards (preventing fabricated ~100% PnL) MUST be retained.
rationale: Findings §7.1 L330. Core parity-critical exit ordering; reordering flips which level closes and the recorded PnL.

text: The within-candle rule-evaluation ORDER MUST stay fixed: funding (8h boundary) → per-position liq/TP/SL → equity rules → trailing profit → time rules — verified by a fixture where two rules would fire in the same candle and only the first-in-order close is recorded.
rationale: Findings §7.1 L331. Reordering changes which rule closes first → different PnL.

text: The equity-rule cascade within a tick MUST stay EQUITY_DROP(/SMART) → close_on_profit → EQUITY_RISE, each with early return on cycle termination, so a config arming multiple equity rules closes via the highest-precedence one only.
rationale: Findings §7.1 L332. Portfolio-level cascade ordering.

text: EQUITY_DROP_PCT_SMART MUST remain one-shot per scan (`smart_drawdown_fired`): it closes ONLY intrabar-losing positions, does a conditional re-anchor, and the fired flag MUST reset on each non-skipped scan (the re-arm) — tested by a two-scan fixture asserting it fires again after re-arm.
rationale: Findings §7.1 L338, Discovery §8 L120. Named landmine; the one-shot + re-arm is the subtle part.

text: Non-smart equity rules MUST zero `cycle_start_equity` to terminate the cycle and re-anchor to `totalAvailableBalance` on each non-skipped scan — distinct from the SMART variant's conditional re-anchor — verified by asserting cycle termination on the non-smart path.
rationale: Findings §7.1 L339. Cycle-termination latch differs between smart and non-smart.

text: Drill-down MUST be triggered if and only if ≥2 of {liquidation, SL, TP} fall inside the same 5m bar (and the entry+its-stop-both-inside case); single-level bars MUST stay resolved exactly at 5m — the trigger condition may be neither broadened nor narrowed.
rationale: Findings §5.2 L274, §7.1 L333. The mandatory-drill predicate is exact, not heuristic.

text: The coarse-pass touch test for TP/SL/entry MUST use the bar's High/Low (never close), preserving the conservative-bound guarantee that an untouched [low,high] range provably skips the drill with zero accuracy loss.
rationale: Findings §5.1 L269, §5.5 L289. Using close instead of H/L would miss intrabar touches.

text: When drilling, 1m sub-bars MUST be replayed in chronological order and the FIRST touched level returned, applying the pessimistic liq→sl→tp precedence within each individual 1m candle.
rationale: Findings §7.1 L333, §5.5 L291. Time-ordered first-touch is the accuracy mechanism.

text: On residual ambiguity (both TP and SL inside the SAME 1m sub-bar), the engine MUST apply the conservative SL-first tie-break — and a test MUST assert this resolution is never more optimistic than (PnL ≤) an always-LTF oracle on the same data.
rationale: Findings §5.4 L283, §5.5 L291, §7.2 L362. Removes optimistic bias; keeps within the <1% safe side.

text: Portfolio equity rules MUST value uPnL off the stable `equity_ref_entry` (the un-drilled 5m fill), NOT the drilled entry price, so the drill path can never change which equity threshold fires.
rationale: Findings §7.1 L336, §5.5 L295. Drill-down is price-precision-only for equity selection.

text: 1m ENTRY drill-down MUST stay price-only (refining `entry_base_price`) while `equity_ref_base` is preserved as the un-drilled 5m fill and the entry-bar lifecycle stays on 5m.
rationale: Findings §5.5 L296. Two separate refs (equity_ref_entry / equity_ref_base) both stay un-drilled.

text: Toggling `drilldown_enabled` MUST yield identical trade SELECTION (the same set of positions opened and the same close rules firing in the same order); only intrabar fill PRICE may differ — verified by a drill-on vs drill-off diff on a fixture.
rationale: Findings §5.5 L295. The strongest observable statement of drill parity.

text: The 1m equity fine-walk MUST require full-book coverage (every open symbol has a 1m window for that bar); if any open symbol lacks a 1m window the engine MUST fall back to 5m equity logic (fail-soft), and a no-1m-candles drill MUST fall back to 5m and never fabricate a fill.
rationale: Findings §5.5 L297-298. Two fail-soft rules: missing fill → 5m; partial book → 5m.

text: Intrabar-aware drawdown MUST value each open position at its OWN bar adverse extreme (high for shorts, low for longs), fire on `min(close_equity, intrabar_equity)`, and exit at the adverse extreme on an intrabar breach.
rationale: Findings §7.1 L337. Per-position adverse-extreme valuation is path- and side-dependent.

text: Funding MUST be charged exactly once per 0/8/16h boundary via the `(date, hour)` dedupe key regardless of candle granularity (longs pay, shorts receive) — and a drill-down fixture MUST assert the finer granularity does not double-charge funding.
rationale: Findings §7.1 L342. Granularity-independent dedupe is the drill landmine here.

text: The TRAILING_PROFIT state machine MUST be preserved exactly: clear peak when uPnL ≤ 0; below activation continue WITHOUT triggering while preserving the peak; peak taken from bar high (Buy)/low (Sell); trigger when `per_unit < peak × 0.5`.
rationale: Findings §7.1 L343. Stateful latch with side-dependent peak source.

text: Time-rule ordering MUST stay MR `time_stop_minutes` → MAX_DURATION → BREAKEVEN_TIMEOUT, where BREAKEVEN_TIMEOUT mutates TP to breakeven and is skipped while trailing is active.
rationale: Findings §7.1 L344. MR time-stop precedes the generic time rules; breakeven/trailing interaction.

text: The wallet/liquidation accounting MUST be preserved: locked margin is NEVER deducted at open (only the entry fee), a normal close adds `wallet_delta`, and liquidation deducts `locked_margin` via a separate branch with NO exit slippage and NO exit fee.
rationale: Findings §7.1 L345. Liquidation is a distinct accounting branch.

text: The liquidation fixture MUST deliberately omit SL or place it outside the liquidation band, because the SL-clamp pulls SL inside the band and makes `close_reason='liquidation'` otherwise unreachable in normal configs.
rationale: Discovery §8 L119. Without this, liquidation parity is never actually exercised.

text: Position sizing MUST stay `qty = (sizing_capital · capital_pct/100 · leverage) / entry_base_price` (un-slipped mark), floored to `qty_step`, rejecting `qty < min_qty` and rejecting when required margin > available_balance — with the rounding/flooring producing bit-identical qty.
rationale: Findings §7.1 L327. Sizing rounding is a named extra-care surface.

text: `sizing_capital` MUST remain path-dependent: running wallet + carried uPnL − Σ locked_margin, marked to the last close ≤ current_time — and the SoA/searchsorted mark MUST reproduce that exact mark price.
rationale: Findings §7.1 L327. The mark source for sizing is the same 'last close ≤ T' that searchsorted must reproduce.

text: Entry fill MUST remain next-bar-open with no look-ahead: fill at the OPEN of the first candle whose `open_time >= current_time`; `searchsorted(side='left')` MUST reproduce the exact `>=` boundary; when none exists, increment `signals_no_kline` and SKIP (never fabricate a fill from a stale close).
rationale: Findings §7.1 L326, RC-12. The side='left' boundary is the parity contract for the SoA refactor.

text: The 'last close ≤ T' lookup via `searchsorted(side='right')-1` and the never-resetting merge-walk pointers MUST reproduce the current linear-prefix mark-seeding result bit-identically (RC-1/RC-2), proven by a diff over a multi-scan carried-position fixture.
rationale: Findings RC-1 L37-39, RC-2 L41-46. The biggest engine change must be selection-neutral.

text: int64-epoch conversion MUST accept both datetime and epoch `open_time` inputs without altering any ordering or comparison, and the float64 OHLC representation MUST introduce no rounding that flips any threshold (TP/SL/equity/drift) decision versus the current engine — proven by byte-identical fixtures.
rationale: Findings RC-11 L95-98 (open_time may be datetime or epoch); float64 vs current math is a rounding-parity risk.

text: The float summation order for portfolio equity (Σ uPnL across the open book) MUST be deterministic and identical between old and new engines, and the numba kernel MUST NOT enable `fastmath` or any flag that reorders/associates float operations.
rationale: Findings §4.2 numba rules L227-232. Equity threshold firing is sensitive to float-sum order; fastmath breaks bit-parity.

text: On EVERY fixture, `trade.pnl = pnl − exit_fee − entry_fee − funding_paid` (TradingView Net Profit) AND the reconciliation `Σ trade.pnl == final_equity − starting_capital` MUST hold — an explicit per-trade-sum cross-check, not just `metrics['net_profit'] == final_equity − start`.
rationale: Findings §7.1 L346, §7.2 L363, Discovery §7 L108. Existing `_assert_reconciles` lacks the per-trade sum; must be added.

text: MFE/MAE MUST be updated every bar from that bar's high/low BEFORE the exit checks run, so the recorded excursions match the current engine on every trade.
rationale: Findings §7.1 L347. Ordering (update before exit) is the subtlety.

text: End-of-backtest force-close MUST close all remaining positions at each symbol's last close with reason `backtest_end`, emit an equity-curve point on every close, and the terminal equity point MUST equal `wallet_balance`.
rationale: Findings §7.1 L348. Terminal reconciliation + curve completeness.

text: The 17-step filter chain MUST execute in the exact existing order, with `abs(score)` ranking, existing-symbol dedup, `max_trades` per-scan (`scan_entered`), and `signals_entered` lifetime counting for `target_goal` all preserved — verified by a fixture that would change selection if any step were reordered.
rationale: Findings §7.1 L352, Discovery §3 L46-48, §11 L159. Named extra-care surface.

text: `max_same_sector` MUST remain an intentional no-op in the engine (do NOT add enforcement), and the service MUST keep emitting the `max_same_sector_not_enforced` warning.
rationale: Findings §7.1 L353, Discovery §8 L122. Explicit do-not-fix divergence vs live.

text: `skip_if_positions_open` MUST skip the scan's signals while STILL evaluating close rules, and MUST preserve the existing anchor (the only anchor-preservation case) rather than re-anchoring.
rationale: Findings §7.1 L351. Anchor preservation is unique to this gate.

text: The golden no-op guarantee MUST hold after every phase: with empty `instrument_info`/`scan_contexts`/`fine_klines` and no regime feature active, the engine output is byte-identical to the pure 5m path — all drill/regime/instrument refinements stay gated behind presence-of-data checks.
rationale: Findings §7.1 L355-356, Discovery §8 L124. The cheapest, strongest CI guard.

text: On the canonical 5m-only no-drill config the optimized engine MUST produce BYTE-IDENTICAL trades AND full equity_curve to the frozen stored-snapshot oracle, run as a CI gate at every phase (no magic-number re-derivation).
rationale: Findings §7.2 L361, Discovery §7 L105-106. Prefer stored snapshot over inline magic numbers.

text: On drill/portfolio configs, per-trade fields and every summary metric MUST deviate <1% from the oracle AND be non-optimistic (the optimized/fast-path PnL must be ≤ the sequential oracle on ambiguous bars), with the <1% measured against the always-LTF replay where applicable.
rationale: Findings §7.2 L362, §5.6 L300. Defines both the tolerance and the directional (non-optimistic) constraint.

text: The optional vectorized barrier-exit fast-path MUST be gated EXACTLY on `max_drawdown_pct >= 100` AND not `close_on_profit` AND `target_goal_type != 'profit_pct'` AND not `trailing_profit_pct` AND no breakeven mutation; any config outside this subset MUST route to the sequential kernel, validated config-by-config against it.
rationale: Findings §4.2 L234, Phase 6 L398. An over-broad gate would silently break path-dependent parity.

text: Parallel sweep execution (semaphore-bounded `asyncio.gather` and/or ProcessPool `initializer`-shipped snapshot) MUST yield per-combo metrics identical to serial execution, with `(sweep_id, config_hash)` idempotency and the set-based rank UPDATE leaving the metrics rows themselves unchanged.
rationale: Findings RC-13/14/15, §4.4 L256-257. Parallelism and rank-stamping must be result-neutral.

text: The sealed-manifest/columnar loader MUST return byte-identical klines to the current Postgres read for any sealed range; a sealed closed day MUST be fetched exactly once across N reruns (mock the Bybit client, assert call count == 1); the forming day (date ≥ today−1) MUST still be refreshed; `store_klines` stays `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`, `_update_coverage` stays `GREATEST`-monotonic, and any in-process/1m memo is invalidated for the forming day and never persisted to the 5m coverage table.
rationale: Findings §3.2 L185, §7.2 L365, §5.5 L292. Bundles the cache-fix parity contract into one testable block.

text: `metrics.total_trades` MUST always be present and never renamed/retyped/removed (its absence routes completed runs to the 'no trades simulated' fallback); the ~45 metric keys may only be extended with optional nullable keys; and the equity_curve manifest hash MUST hash the FULL stored JSONB, not the LTTB-downsampled GET view.
rationale: Discovery §5 L86-89. The breaking-change trap + downsample hashing pitfall.

text: The stored `equity_curve` ORDER MUST be preserved exactly, because path-dependent metrics (max drawdown, Sharpe, Sortino) are computed over its sequence — verified by hashing the ordered curve, not a set.
rationale: Discovery §6 L99, §11 L159. Reordering silently corrupts path-dependent metrics.

text: The adaptive blacklist (computed from the backtest's OWN trade history) MUST, at scan time T, see exactly the trades closed before T in the same order — the SoA/parallel refactor must not change this point-in-time, path-dependent set (enabled, min_trades=5, max_win_rate=30, lookback_hours=48 semantics unchanged).
rationale: Findings §1/§4.2 'adaptive-blacklist-from-own-trades' latch; Discovery §3 L53-54. A path-dependent latch easy to break under reordering.

text: `execution_mode` (immediate vs batch) and `fill_to_max_trades` MUST each produce results identical to the current engine — both included as distinct fixtures in the battery, since they change how a scan's signals are admitted within a cycle.
rationale: Findings §7.2 L360 fixture battery explicitly lists 'batch vs immediate mode' and 'fill_to_max_trades'.

text: Accepted/known divergences MUST be preserved as-is and asserted by tests: `mr_long_ack` is BYPASSED in backtest, `strategy_cohort` None defaults to trend, and `regime_staleness_minutes` is live-only (backtest builds a fresh ScanContext per scan_time, so it is N/A) — none of these may be 'corrected' during optimization.
rationale: Discovery §3 L58-60, §8 L126. Documented intentional behaviors that an optimizer might naively 'fix'.

text: The completion frontier MUST be computed per (symbol, interval) as floor(now / T) * T, and any candle with open_time < frontier MUST be classified CLOSED/immutable (4h seals later than 5m for the same wall-clock day).
rationale: §3.2(1): a single global frontier would wrongly seal/unseal coarse vs fine TFs; per-(symbol,interval) frontier is the basis of fetch-once-ever.

text: The frontier MUST be recomputed at read time on every run so a day that was the forming/live-edge day on an earlier run automatically becomes sealed on a later run once day.end <= frontier, with no manual reseal step.
rationale: §3.2(1,3): sealing must be a pure function of now; otherwise the forming day stays perpetually refetchable.

text: A per-day manifest row keyed (symbol, interval, day_utc) MUST persist sealed BOOL, first_open_ts, last_open_ts, candle_count, gap_count, listing_snapped, delisted, content_sha256, and fetched_at.
rationale: §3.2(2): the manifest is the new source of truth; these exact columns are required for the seal rule, negative caching, integrity, and TTL.

text: A day MUST be sealed if and only if day.end <= frontier AND an exchange response for that day has been fetched; once sealed it MUST never be re-evaluated or refetched regardless of candle_count.
rationale: §3.2(3): this exact rule kills the '144/288 → refetch forever' bug.

text: candle_count MUST be stored as a fact column only and MUST NOT appear in any fetch-decision predicate; a test MUST assert that a sealed short day (e.g. 144/288) triggers zero refetch.
rationale: RC-3/§3.2(4): the root cause is count entering the gate; removing it is the fix, and the test guards regression.

text: Only the forming candle/day (open_time >= frontier) MAY be refreshed, and the live edge MUST be detected by timestamp, never by candle count (Freqtrade rule).
rationale: §3.2 + RC-3 verifier caveat: detecting incompleteness by timestamp is the proven correct discriminator.

text: A symbol_lifecycle(symbol, listing_time, delist_time) table MUST be persisted and consulted before any fetch so out-of-life ranges are excluded.
rationale: §3.2(2,5): lifecycle bounds are required for both the refetch predicate and negative caching.

text: Pre-listing and post-delist day ranges MUST be recorded as known-empty, sealed permanently, and MUST never be fetched from the exchange.
rationale: §3.2(5) RFC 2308 analogy: confirmed absences must short-circuit before any exchange call.

text: The refetch predicate MUST be exactly need_fetch = (not sealed) AND day_overlaps([listing_time, delist_time]) AND day.end > last_covered, with no count term.
rationale: §3.2(4): the count-free predicate is the precise replacement for the buggy comparator.

text: Re-verified intra-day structural gaps (true exchange gaps confirmed on refetch) MUST be recordable as known-empty so they stop being fetched on subsequent runs.
rationale: §3.2(5): interior structural gaps otherwise behave like perpetual gaps.

text: Only unsealed/transient gap days MAY carry a fetched_at TTL allowing them to heal; the TTL MUST never apply to sealed days.
rationale: §3.2(5): transient mis-records need a heal path, but sealed immutability must be absolute.

text: A listing-day boundary day MUST be marked listing_snapped and its partial candle count accepted as complete (not a gap) once day.end <= frontier.
rationale: RC-3 verifier: listing-day shortness is the unequivocal trigger; clipping must be preserved, not treated as missing.

text: The fetcher MUST issue one request span per contiguous gap-run, never a single min(gap_days)..max(gap_days) span.
rationale: RC-4/§3.2(6): the min..max collapse is the bracket bug; per-run fetch is the structural fix.

text: A regression test MUST reproduce the 'listing day near start + forming day near end → full-history re-download' scenario and assert the fix fetches zero closed-day candles on rerun.
rationale: RC-3+RC-4 is the headline bug; it needs an explicit reproduction lock so it cannot silently return.

text: ensure_coverage MUST be gated behind a manifest 'range already complete' check; a fully-sealed requested range MUST perform zero exchange calls and zero coverage scans.
rationale: RC-5/§3.2(7): unconditional ensure_coverage every run is a HIGH cost even absent the detector bug.

text: The sealed manifest MUST be the single source of truth for 'is this range complete'; the loader MUST consult the manifest rather than issuing a live COUNT(*) query.
rationale: §4.1: count queries are exactly the anti-pattern; manifest lookup is O(1) and authoritative.

text: _load_klines MUST be wrapped in a process-level LRU keyed (symbol, interval, start, end) such that a byte-identical rerun performs zero Bybit fetches and zero Postgres round-trips.
rationale: §3.2(7)/RC-5: cross-run memo is what makes warm reruns near-instant.

text: The LRU/hot cache MUST memoize sealed days only; the unsealed tail/forming day MUST never be memoized and MUST always be re-read fresh.
rationale: §3.2(7): memoizing the forming day would serve stale, mutable data.

text: Any LRU/memo or columnar read hit MUST yield klines byte-identical to a fresh Postgres row read, asserted by a golden test before the path is trusted.
rationale: §4.1 parity guarantee: the cache changes HOW data is read, never WHAT bytes the engine sees.

text: The in-process LRU/hot cache MUST be bounded by entry-count and/or byte budget with eviction so long sweeps cannot exhaust memory.
rationale: §4.1 sizing (~50–100MB working set) is small, but unbounded growth across many sweep combos risks OOM.

text: The shared hot/LRU cache MUST be safe under _MAX_CONCURRENT=3 concurrent runs with no torn reads and no race on insert/evict.
rationale: §4 of discovery: 3 concurrent backtests share the process; a shared mutable cache needs thread/async safety.

text: A per-session in-process 1m drill cache keyed (symbol, bar_open_epoch) MUST make a rerun re-pull zero 1m candles for already-seen bars.
rationale: RC-7/§3.2(8): the 1m drill is re-pulled in full every run; the per-bar memo removes it on warm reruns.

text: The 1m drill cache MUST be in-memory only and MUST never call store_klines, so it cannot re-poison the 5m coverage table.
rationale: RC-7/§3.2(8): persisting 1m would create false short days in the 5m coverage manifest.

text: Drill-down MUST fetch only narrow per-bar windows (grouping adjacent epochs), never a contiguous min(epochs)→max(epochs) span that discards ~98% of candles.
rationale: RC-7: the contiguous span is the over-fetch source and the >16.6h pagination-truncation hazard.

text: The durable read store MUST be immutable Parquet in a hive layout symbol=<SYM>/year=<Y>/month=<M>/part.parquet, sorted by open_time within each file so row-group min/max stats prune date ranges.
rationale: §4.1(1): symbol→month partitioning + intra-file sort enables predicate/partition pushdown.

text: Partition granularity MUST be month, not day, to avoid small-file sprawl (~4,500 tiny files for 90d × 50 symbols).
rationale: §4.1(1): per-day partitioning is explicitly rejected for file-count overhead.

text: All Parquet/Feather writes MUST be atomic (temp-write + rename) so a concurrent reader never observes a partially written file.
rationale: §4.1 immutability: write-once columnar files must never expose torn reads to a sweep slicing in parallel.

text: The bulk OHLCV read path MUST use DuckDB read_parquet or Polars scan_parquet with column + predicate + partition pushdown, reading only requested columns over the requested date range.
rationale: §4.1(2): columnar pushdown is the ~1–2 order-of-magnitude cold-read win vs asyncpg row scan.

text: An in-process hot layer keyed {(symbol, interval, date-range): Arrow/Polars frame} MUST serve warm sweep reruns entirely from RAM with no disk or wire access.
rationale: §4.1(3): the working set fits in RAM, so warm reruns should be RAM slices.

text: Cross-process reruns MUST be served from mmap'd Feather V2 (Arrow IPC, zero deserialization), and the mmap for the forming day MUST be invalidated so a separate process never serves a stale live edge.
rationale: §4.1(3): mmap'd Feather gives instant separate-process reruns; forming-day invalidation preserves correctness.

text: PostgreSQL MUST remain the write-of-record for ingest/coverage/reconciliation; Parquet/DuckDB/Arrow files MUST be a derived READ cache only, regenerable from Postgres.
rationale: §4.1: keep ACID ingest on Postgres; columnar is a disposable read accelerator.

text: store_klines MUST stay ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING and _update_coverage MUST stay GREATEST-monotonic even with the manifest layered on top.
rationale: §3.2 parity guarantee: existing idempotency/monotonic invariants must not regress.

text: The columnar read path MUST return klines byte-identical (same epoch ints, same OHLCV float bytes, same ordering) to the Postgres row read, gated by a dedicated test before it is enabled.
rationale: §4.1: a new storage path is only trustworthy once proven equal to the system-of-record read.

text: Exactly ONE base granularity MUST be stored, and coarse timeframes (15m/1h/4h) MUST be derived from the sealed fine base via time_bucket / group_by_dynamic rather than fetched/stored per-TF.
rationale: §4.1 + Phase 5: storing per-TF multiplies storage and seal bookkeeping; derive coarse from sealed fine.

text: Derived coarse candles MUST equal exchange-native coarse candles within the parity tolerance, asserted by a validation test on a sample range.
rationale: Aggregation correctness (boundary alignment, partial buckets) must be proven, not assumed.

text: A content_sha256 MUST be stored per sealed day, and a sealed day MAY be refetched ONLY on an explicit hash-mismatch integrity escape hatch (never on count).
rationale: §3.2(2): the hash provides corruption detection without reintroducing count-based refetch.

text: Closed candles MUST be fetch-once-ever and partial-day clipping for boundary days (listing day, current day) MUST be preserved exactly as today.
rationale: §3.2 parity guarantee: immutability and boundary clipping are both correctness invariants.

text: Sealed-manifest columns/tables MUST ship as migration v58 (next free int after v57) using ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS with constant defaults, fully idempotent.
rationale: §9: v58 is the claimed next free int; idempotent additive DDL avoids the prior version-collision incidents.

text: Any multi-statement sealed-manifest DDL MUST be implemented as a CALLABLE migration, never a single semicolon-delimited string.
rationale: §9 hazard (b): the migration runner splits string DDL on inner ';' incorrectly.

text: A one-time backfill MUST set sealed=true for every day whose end < current frontier AND that lies within [listing_time, delist_time], and MUST ensure a partition exists for any sealed range older than ±6 months (else rows land in unpruned kline_cache_default).
rationale: §3.2(9) + §9 hazard (c): backfill makes existing short-but-complete days stop refetching immediately, and the ±6mo partition window must be extended for old sealed ranges.

text: pyarrow and duckdb MUST be import-guarded so a missing wheel falls back to the Postgres read path and never breaks backend import; the columnar read path MUST be feature-flagged for rollback to identical Postgres-read results.
rationale: §10 + Rollback: the backend is currently zero-heavy-dep; a missing/incompatible wheel must degrade gracefully, and the new read path must be reversible without behavior change.

text: The sealed-manifest migration MUST be registered as version integer 58 in the _MIGRATIONS list in backend/async_persistence.py, and a test MUST assert there is exactly one migration with version 58 and no duplicate/colliding version int in the list.
rationale: Discovery §9: current latest = v57, next free = v58; version-int collisions from parallel branches have happened twice before.

text: Migration v58 MUST be implemented as a Python callable (not a multi-statement DDL string), verified by a test that the callable executes every statement separately against a connection rather than relying on string-splitting.
rationale: Discovery §9 hazard (b): multi-statement DDL passed as a string is split on inner ';' incorrectly — a callable migration is required.

text: Every ALTER TABLE in v58 MUST use ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS with a constant (non-volatile) DEFAULT, and CREATE TABLE statements MUST use CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, proven idempotent by invoking the migration callable twice against the same DB with identical resulting schema and no error.
rationale: Discovery §9: sealed columns are net-new, must be ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS with constant default, idempotent; symbol_lifecycle is a new table.

text: The sealed `sealed` column MUST default to a constant FALSE (not a computed/volatile expression) so ADD COLUMN does not trigger a full table rewrite or lock on kline_cache_coverage.
rationale: Findings §3.2 step 2 adds sealed BOOL; constant default avoids table-rewrite lock on the large coverage table.

text: Migration v58 MUST add the full sealed-manifest column set (sealed BOOL, first_open_ts, last_open_ts, gap_count, listing_snapped, delisted, content_sha256, fetched_at) to kline_cache_coverage, each nullable or constant-default, verified present and correctly typed by a post-migration schema introspection test.
rationale: Findings §3.2 step 2 enumerates the per-day manifest fields; candle_count stays a FACT, never a gate.

text: Migration v58 MUST create the symbol_lifecycle(symbol, listing_time, delist_time) table idempotently, verified by a test asserting the table and columns exist after one and after two migration runs.
rationale: Findings §3.2 step 2 requires a symbol_lifecycle table for the count-free refetch predicate and negative caching.

text: The one-time backfill MUST set sealed = true for every (symbol, interval, day) whose day.end < the current per-interval completion frontier AND that lies within [listing_time, delist_time], verified by a test where a historic short-but-complete day (e.g. 144/288) is sealed after migration.
rationale: Findings §3.2 step 9: backfill seal for historic short-but-complete days stops the 144/288 refetch-forever bug immediately.

text: The backfill MUST NOT seal the still-forming current day (day.end >= frontier), verified by a test asserting today's partial/last day remains sealed = false after migration.
rationale: Findings §3.2 steps 1/3/parity-guarantee: only date >= today-1 (the forming candle) may be refreshed; the frontier defines closed vs. forming.

text: The backfill MUST be re-runnable/idempotent: an interrupted backfill, when the migration is re-invoked, completes the seal with no double effect, verified by a test that runs the backfill twice and asserts identical sealed rows and counts.
rationale: Idempotency contract for ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS migrations; partial-failure recovery on deploy.

text: The backfill MUST execute as a bounded/set-based UPDATE (or chunked commits) rather than an unbounded row-by-row loop, with a test asserting it completes within a defined statement budget on a representative coverage volume so it cannot lock kline_cache_coverage indefinitely on large histories.
rationale: kline_cache_coverage can be large; an unbounded backfill in one transaction risks long locks at deploy time.

text: The schema_version row MUST be advanced to 58 only after all v58 DDL and the seal backfill succeed within the migration's transaction, verified by a test that an injected mid-migration failure leaves schema_version at 57 with no partial column/table left applied (or fully rolled back).
rationale: Discovery §9: single schema_version row; atomic apply prevents a half-applied v58 from blocking re-run.

text: The sealed manifest and read path MUST correctly handle ranges older than ±6 months from the original migration-38 partition window that live in kline_cache_default, verified by a test that seals and reads a >6mo-old date range stored in the DEFAULT partition.
rationale: Discovery §9 hazard (c): partition set only spans ±6mo from migration time; >6mo-old sealed ranges may live in kline_cache_default.

text: A backtest requesting a >6mo-old range MUST read klines correctly without assuming a dedicated monthly partition exists, verified by an integration test loading a date range ~8 months in the past.
rationale: Discovery §9: monthly partitions only ±6mo; loader must not assume a pruned partition for old ranges.

text: Storing sealed-day klines for a historic (>6mo-old) range MUST auto-create the needed monthly partition or fall through to kline_cache_default without error, verified by a test that store_klines persists a candle dated >6mo ago.
rationale: Discovery §9: store_klines auto-creates partitions on write; old ranges otherwise land in DEFAULT.

text: numba, pyarrow, and duckdb MUST be declared in pyproject.toml [project].dependencies with `>=` floor pins under requires-python >= 3.10, verified by a test that parses pyproject and asserts each dependency and floor is present.
rationale: Discovery §10: declare in pyproject [project].dependencies, `>=` floor, requires-python ≥3.10.

text: Each of numba, pyarrow, and duckdb MUST be import-guarded independently (try/except ImportError) so that a missing wheel for any one does NOT crash backend import nor disable the other optimizations, verified by tests that simulate each module absent and assert backend.main still imports.
rationale: Discovery §10: backend is currently pure-Python with zero heavy deps; a missing wheel must not make the entire backend fail to import; per-feature guards isolate failures.

text: Every import-guarded optimization MUST have a pure-Python/numpy fallback that produces golden-master-identical output, verified by a test that forces the dependency absent and asserts the backtest result is byte-identical to the oracle on the canonical 5m no-drill path.
rationale: Discovery §10 + findings golden-master parity; numba is optional and Phase 3 must hit 'minutes' without it.

text: The numba JIT path MUST verify numba/llvmlite/numpy version compatibility at import and, on an incompatible combination (e.g. numba lacking a wheel for Python 3.14.3 / numpy 2.4.4), disable the JIT and fall back rather than raise, verified by a test simulating a mismatched numpy version.
rationale: Discovery §10 D5 risk: Python 3.14.3 + numpy 2.4.4 is bleeding-edge for numba 0.65.1/llvmlite 0.47.0.

text: The no-numba (pure-Python/numpy SoA) path MUST independently meet the run-time budget (well under the 120s timeout / 'minutes' target), verified by a perf test with numba forced absent.
rationale: Discovery §10: 'Phase 3 must hit minutes so numba is optional'; deploy targets without a numba wheel must still pass.

text: First-call numba JIT compile latency (1–10s) MUST NOT consume the 120s run deadline — the kernel MUST be warmed before the timed run or compile excluded from the deadline — verified by a cold-start run test that completes under the timeout.
rationale: Discovery §4/§10 and findings §4.2: first-call JIT compile is 1–10s; the 120s timeout is a hard wall-clock cap.

text: numba cache=True artifacts MUST be written to a writable location or degrade gracefully when the cache dir is read-only, verified by a test running the JIT path with a read-only __pycache__/cache dir.
rationale: Findings §4.2 mandates cache=True to amortize compile; read-only deploy filesystems must not break the path.

text: The newly added heavy deps MUST be incorporated into the project's pip-audit/CVE floor tracking, verified by a CI step that runs pip-audit including numba/pyarrow/duckdb and fails on a floor violation.
rationale: Discovery §10: project tracks transitive CVEs via pip-audit floors; new deps widen that surface.

text: CI MUST verify that prebuilt wheels (not source builds) for numba, llvmlite, pyarrow, and duckdb install at the pinned versions on the deploy target's OS/arch/Python, failing the build if any resolves to a source build.
rationale: Discovery §10 deploy-target wheel availability; bleeding-edge Python may lack wheels for numba/llvmlite.

text: Each optimization (sealed-manifest cache gate, batched ANY($1) loaders, SoA engine, numba kernel, Parquet/DuckDB read layer, vectorized barrier fast-path, parallel-sweep outer loop) MUST be independently toggleable via an env var / config flag with a documented safe default, verified by tests exercising each flag both on and off.
rationale: Rollback safety requires per-optimization toggles; phased rollout (findings §8) treats phases as independently shippable.

text: A numba kill-switch env var (e.g. BACKTEST_DISABLE_NUMBA) MUST force the pure-Python kernel without code changes, verified by a test asserting the switch routes execution to the fallback and matches the oracle.
rationale: Findings §8 Phase 4 risk medium-high; an operational kill-switch lets ops disable JIT if it misbehaves in prod.

text: A Parquet/DuckDB read-layer kill-switch MUST revert the backtest READ path to Postgres (system-of-record) with no data loss, verified by a test asserting reads return byte-identical klines with the flag off.
rationale: Findings §4.1: Postgres stays write-of-record; the columnar read layer is gated by a byte-identical klines test (Phase 5 risk medium).

text: The sealed-manifest gate MUST be toggleable so disabling it restores the legacy get_coverage_gaps coverage path, verified by a test that with the flag off the legacy count-based detection runs unchanged.
rationale: Phase 1 is low-risk but a revert path is required if the manifest misbehaves on a real symbol's lifecycle.

text: The vectorized barrier fast-path MUST have a flag that, when off, routes ALL configs (even eligible independent-position ones) to the sequential kernel, verified by a test asserting flag-off output equals sequential-kernel output.
rationale: Findings §4.2/§8 Phase 6: route anything ambiguous to the sequential kernel; a global off-switch protects against a bad eligibility gate.

text: The feature-flag defaults for the first production deploy MUST be documented and asserted (which phases ship enabled vs. dark), verified by a test that the shipped config defaults match the documented staged-rollout matrix.
rationale: Staged rollout (findings §8 lowest-risk-first) requires explicit, testable default posture per phase.

text: Rolling the application code back to the pre-optimization version MUST run correctly against the v58 schema (additive columns/tables ignored by old code), verified by a test that exercises the legacy code path against a v58-migrated DB.
rationale: v58 is additive-only; a code rollback must not hit missing/changed columns. Migrations are forward-only with a single schema_version row.

text: Migration v58 MUST NOT drop, rename, or retype any existing kline_cache, kline_cache_coverage, or backtest_* column, verified by a schema-diff test asserting v58 only adds columns/tables.
rationale: Discovery §9: only ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS; preserving existing columns keeps code rollback safe and keeps persisted-result reads intact.

text: A runtime failure in the numba, DuckDB, or Parquet path MUST be caught and degrade to the verified pure-Python/Postgres path within the same run rather than failing the backtest, verified by a test that injects a runtime error into each accelerated path and asserts the run completes with oracle-identical results.
rationale: Rollback-in-place protects production when an accelerated dependency misbehaves at runtime, not just at import.

text: With ALL optimization flags off, the system MUST reproduce the legacy code path byte-for-byte (golden master) on the canonical 5m no-drill path, verified by an 'all-flags-off == oracle' test.
rationale: Findings §7.2/§8: golden-master parity is the safety net; the fully-disabled state is the trusted rollback baseline.

text: There MUST be a tested, documented procedure to clear/rebuild the Parquet/Arrow/Feather read cache and the in-process _load_klines LRU without touching Postgres, verified by a test that corrupts/clears the cache and asserts a rebuild restores correct reads.
rationale: Findings §4.1/§4.3: derived columnar + in-process caches; a corrupt/stale derived cache must be recoverable independent of the system-of-record.

text: The in-process LRU and the 1m drill-down in-memory cache MUST be invalidatable for the forming (unsealed) day so neither serves a stale tail across a deploy/rollback, verified by a test asserting the forming-day entry is refreshed while sealed days remain memoized.
rationale: Findings §3.2 steps 7/8 and parity guarantee: memoize sealed days only; the 1m cache stays in-memory and must never re-poison the 5m coverage table.

text: Persisted backtest_results MUST always include metrics.total_trades and all ~45 existing metric keys with unchanged names/types, verified by a test asserting the new engine's persisted result JSON contains every legacy key (and that total_trades is present/non-renamed).
rationale: Discovery §5: if metrics.total_trades is absent/renamed the UI routes completed runs to the 'no trades simulated' fallback (BacktestResultsPage.tsx:255).

text: Pre-optimization persisted backtest_results/backtest_trades rows MUST remain readable and renderable by the new code and GET endpoints without migrating historic result rows, and any new metrics/manifest fields MUST be added as nullable, verified by a test loading an old result row with NULL new-fields.
rationale: Discovery §5: result/trade JSON is a backward-compat surface — only ADD optional nullable keys; never rename/retype/remove.

text: Persistence idempotency MUST be preserved across the change — results upsert ON CONFLICT(run_id), trades delete-before-insert, kline INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING, coverage GREATEST upsert — verified by a test that re-persisting the same run yields identical stored rows.
rationale: Discovery §5 + findings §3.2 parity guarantee: store_klines stays idempotent and _update_coverage stays monotonic (GREATEST).

text: The GET /backtest-cache/status response MUST remain additive-only, preserving CacheStatusResponse{symbols_total, symbols_cached, symbols_with_gaps, ready}; any sealed-manifest signal MUST be a new optional field, verified by a contract test against the existing shape.
rationale: Discovery §4/§5: cache-status is a frontend contract; sealed-manifest visibility must not retype/remove existing keys.

text: Migration v58 MUST log start/finish, number of days sealed in the backfill, and the per-interval completion frontier used, verified by a test asserting these are emitted on a fresh migration run.
rationale: Deploy auditability of the seal backfill; the per-interval frontier (findings §3.2 step 1) determines what got sealed.

text: Each fallback/degradation activation (numba unavailable or disabled, duckdb/pyarrow unavailable, fast-path rejected, manifest gate disabled) MUST emit a structured log or metric, and a one-time boot self-check MUST report which optimizations are active vs. degraded, verified by tests asserting each signal and the boot report.
rationale: Operators need to see which code path is live in production after a deploy/rollback when optimizations silently fall back.

text: The migration runner MUST surface a clear, actionable error (not a silent partial apply) if v58 fails on the deploy target — e.g. partition-missing or permission error — verified by a test asserting a failing DDL raises with the version int and failing statement identified.
rationale: Discovery §9 hazards: partial application and version collisions; a loud, identifiable failure is required for safe deploy/rollback decisions.

text: Add a golden fixture exercising the EQUITY_RISE_PCT / close_on_profit basket-flatten close rule (whole open book flattened on a basket-equity threshold) with a frozen stored-snapshot oracle.
rationale: EQUITY_RISE is a portfolio-level close rule named in CLAUDE.md; cross-sectional flatten can't be derived per-symbol and is easy to break silently during SoA/numba refactors.

text: Add a golden fixture exercising the EQUITY_DROP_PCT (max_drawdown_pct) basket-flatten close rule against a stored-snapshot oracle.
rationale: Discovery §3 lists EQUITY_DROP as a portfolio-level latch acting on basket equity = wallet + Σ uPnL; must be pinned independently of the smart variant.

text: Add a golden fixture for EQUITY_DROP_PCT_SMART that asserts it fires exactly once within a scan AND re-arms on the next scan (engine L1139), not just that it fires.
rationale: Discovery §8 flags equity_drop_smart as a one-shot-per-scan latch with re-arm; a fixture that only checks a single fire would pass even if the re-arm regressed.

text: Add a golden fixture for the BREAKEVEN_TIMEOUT close rule (breakeven_timeout_hours) verifying SL is mutated to breakeven after the timeout and the subsequent exit price reflects it.
rationale: breakeven mutation is one of the path-dependent latches and is also a Phase-6 fast-path disqualifier — it needs its own oracle so both kernels can be checked against it.

text: Add a golden fixture for the MAX_DURATION close rule (max_trade_duration_hours) that force-closes a still-open position at the duration limit.
rationale: Time-based force-close is a distinct branch from price barriers; CLAUDE.md enumerates MAX_DURATION as a required close rule.

text: Add a golden fixture for TRAILING_PROFIT (trailing_profit_pct) that ratchets the trail up and exits on retracement, with the ratchet path frozen in the oracle.
rationale: Trailing is path-dependent (ratchet state) and a fast-path disqualifier; CLAUDE.md lists TRAILING_PROFIT as a required close rule.

text: Add single-barrier fixtures for a clean take_profit_pct hit and a clean stop_loss_pct hit (no double-touch), each with a stored oracle.
rationale: The simplest TP/SL branches are the regression backbone; they must be pinned before drilldown/SoA changes the price-location code.

text: Add a golden fixture for target_goal_type='trade_count' that asserts the LIFETIME early-stop halts the whole run at the trade-count goal.
rationale: Discovery §3 marks trade_count as a lifetime early-stop; early termination changes the equity_curve length and every path-dependent metric.

text: Add a golden fixture for target_goal_type='profit_pct' (with required target_goal_value) early-stop.
rationale: profit_pct goal is both a close path and a Phase-6 fast-path disqualifier; needs an independent oracle from trade_count.

text: Add a drilldown fixture where BOTH TP and SL fall inside one coarse bar (mandatory drill) and assert the conservative SL-first tie-break is applied on any residual same-sub-bar ambiguity.
rationale: Findings §5.2/§5.4: both-levels-in-bar is the only case coarse OHLC can't resolve; the worst-case SL-first tie-break is the documented parity rule and must be enforced, not the optimistic open→high→low→close heuristic.

text: Add drilldown fixtures for the AVOIDABLE branches — coarse bar touches neither level (mark-at-close, byte-identical to the no-drill path) and touches exactly one level (fill-at-level, no drill) — and assert no LTF fetch occurs.
rationale: Findings §5.2: the conservative-bound guarantee means these branches must skip the drill with zero accuracy loss; a regression that over-drills or shifts the fill must fail.

text: Add a NO-OP guarantee test asserting that with empty instrument_info, empty scan_contexts, empty fine_klines and regime disabled, engine output is byte-identical to the pure 5m no-drill path, and re-run it after EVERY phase.
rationale: Discovery §8 names this the golden no-op guarantee; it is the canonical bit-identical invariant the whole optimization is gated on.

text: Add a mean-reversion cohort golden fixture (mr_short/mr_long_enabled, mr_tight_stop_pct, mr_time_stop_minutes, mr_mean_period/interval) with mr_long_ack bypass behavior frozen.
rationale: Discovery §3 (F2/F3): MR is a separate cohort with its own sizing/leverage/time-stop and a backtest-only ack bypass; without a fixture the MR path is untested under refactor.

text: Add paired fixtures for execution_mode='immediate' vs 'batch' that produce DISTINCT frozen oracles, proving the two scheduling modes are not accidentally collapsed.
rationale: Config surface lists execution_mode as immediate/batch; batching changes trade open ordering and therefore equity_curve order and path-dependent metrics.

text: Add a force-close fixture where open positions are flattened at cycle termination / end-of-run, asserting every position closes with the correct close_reason and the per-trade-sum still reconciles.
rationale: cycle_start_equity zeroing / end-of-run flush is a terminal path that must leave Σtrade.pnl == final_equity − starting_capital intact.

text: Add a DELIBERATE liquidation fixture that omits SL (or sets SL outside the liquidation band) so close_reason='liquidation' is actually reached, and freeze its oracle.
rationale: Discovery §8: SL-clamp makes liquidation unreachable in normal configs; the only way to test the liquidation branch is to deliberately defeat the clamp.

text: Replace the inline magic-number assertions with a stored-snapshot oracle that captures the FULL result: all 19 BacktestTrade fields + strategy_kind, the ordered equity_curve, all ~45 metrics, summary, and warnings.
rationale: Discovery §7: current golden uses brittle hand-verified constants (38.9725, -510.725, 959.0, -878.4, 77.941103); a full-result snapshot is the robust oracle the spec recommends.

text: Provide a documented, git-tracked oracle-regeneration script that stamps the engine git SHA + config hash into each snapshot, forbids hand-editing, and extends the duplicated _config/_signal/_klines builders IN PLACE rather than consolidating them.
rationale: Discovery §7 warns that the branch builders have different defaults across golden/engine/regime tests and naive consolidation shifts frozen values; provenance stamping makes intentional regenerations auditable.

text: Add an explicit reconciliation cross-check on EVERY fixture asserting the three-way identity Σ trade['pnl'] == metrics['net_profit'] == final_equity − starting_capital within a Decimal tolerance.
rationale: Discovery §7: _assert_reconciles ties only net_profit to final_equity−start and never sums per-trade pnl; the missing per-trade-sum check is the one cross-check that catches silently-dropped or double-counted trades.

text: Assert path-dependent metrics (Sharpe, Sortino, max_dd_pct) by comparing the equity_curve as an ORDERED sequence (not a set/multiset), so any reordering introduced by SoA/parallelism fails.
rationale: Discovery §6: max drawdown and the risk ratios depend on equity_curve order; SoA timeline merge and parallel sweeps are exactly the changes that could permute it.

text: Add a 'sealed day fetched exactly once' test: mock the Bybit fetch, run the same backtest N times, and assert the fetch mock call_count == 1 cold and 0 on warm reruns for every sealed day.
rationale: Findings §3.2: the sealed-manifest fix's core promise is fetch-once-ever for closed days; call_count is the direct, unambiguous assertion of it.

text: Add a test that a legitimately-short sealed day (e.g. a 144/288 listing day) is NEVER refetched on rerun, asserting candle_count never enters the fetch decision (count-free predicate).
rationale: Findings RC-3/§3.2: the original bug is 144<288 staying a perpetual gap; the fix must prove the count is a fact, not a gate.

text: Add a test that the forming/current day (date >= today−1, unsealed tail) IS still refreshed on rerun, so sealing does not freeze live data.
rationale: Findings §3.2 parity guarantee: only the still-forming candle may be refreshed; a test must prove sealing didn't over-reach into the live day.

text: Add a test that fetches occur per contiguous gap-run (not a single min..max span), proving one stale interior day cannot drag the entire window into re-download.
rationale: Findings RC-4: ensure_coverage's min..max span collapse is the second half of the re-download bug; per-gap-run fetch must be asserted directly.

text: Add a memo-correctness test: klines served from the in-process LRU are byte-identical to a fresh Postgres read, an identical rerun does ZERO Bybit and ZERO Postgres kline reads, and the memo is invalidated for the forming day.
rationale: Findings §3.2 item 7 + parity guarantee: the cross-run memo must be transparent (same bytes) and must not cache the unsealed tail.

text: Add a test that the 1m drill-down in-process cache is in-memory ONLY — after a drill, assert the kline_cache / kline_cache_coverage tables are unchanged (no store_klines), so 1m data cannot re-poison 5m coverage.
rationale: Findings §3.1(C)/§3.2 item 8: _build_fine_klines must never persist; persisting 1m would corrupt the 288-per-day coverage expectation.

text: Add a migration v58 test asserting the backfill seals every day with end < frontier within [listing, delist], that the migration is idempotent on re-run, and that it uses ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS with a constant default.
rationale: Discovery §9: v58 is the next free int after v57, version collisions have happened twice, and multi-statement DDL must be a callable; the backfill must immediately stop existing short-day refetch.

text: Add a property/randomized test asserting np.searchsorted-based price location returns the same mark/entry price as the current linear prefix-scan seeding across randomized timelines, including the open_time-may-be-datetime-OR-epoch case.
rationale: Findings RC-1/RC-2 + RC-11 caveat: _fine_window/_sim_bar_seconds already mix datetime and epoch; the SoA int64-epoch conversion is the most likely place to introduce an off-by-one or type-coercion parity break.

text: Add a test asserting the shared next-bar index used for the price-drift check equals the bar used for entry fill (RC-12), so de-duplicating the two linear scans does not shift the fill bar.
rationale: Findings RC-12: _open_position and _apply_filter_chain each do their own linear scan today; sharing one searchsorted result must not change which bar fills.

text: Add a test that runs the full fixture battery through BOTH the pure-Python kernel and the @njit kernel and asserts bit-identical output (deep equality on trades, ordered equity_curve, and all metrics).
rationale: Findings §4.2 / Phase 4: the mandated discipline is develop-in-Python-first, assert oracle-identical, then JIT; the identity assertion is what makes the JIT trustworthy.

text: Add a test that the numba identity test skips cleanly when numba is unavailable (import-guarded) and that the pure-Python fallback path independently passes the entire golden suite, with the JIT warmed once before any timing.
rationale: Discovery §10: numba/pyarrow/duckdb are net-new and must be import-guarded with a pure-Python fallback or the whole backend fails to import; bleeding-edge Py3.14/numpy2.4 makes the fallback the contractual path.

text: Add a test that the Phase-6 vectorized first-touch fast-path output equals the sequential kernel on the eligible subset, plus a property test over config combinations asserting any config with close_on_profit, profit_pct goal, trailing_profit, breakeven mutation, or max_drawdown_pct<100 is ROUTED to the sequential kernel.
rationale: Findings §4.2: the fast-path is only valid when positions are provably independent; the gate's correctness (routing ambiguous configs away) is the real risk, so it needs combinatorial coverage.

text: Add a CI bit-identical guard that runs the full golden suite and FAILS the build on any deviation from the stored oracle, wired to run on every phase's PR.
rationale: Findings closing line: 'Never trust a phase without its golden-master diff'; enforcing it in CI per-phase is how that rule becomes mechanical rather than aspirational.

text: Define and assert an explicit per-path tolerance policy: the canonical 5m no-drill path must be Decimal-exact bit-identical, while float64 SoA/numba paths must stay within a defined epsilon honoring the <1% deviation cap.
rationale: The headline constraint is golden-master bit-identical on the canonical path but <1% elsewhere; tests must encode the two different tolerances rather than applying one loose threshold everywhere.

text: Enforce >=90% line AND branch coverage on backtest_engine.py, backtest_service.py, and kline_cache_service.py in CI, including all 17 filter-chain steps and the max_same_sector no-op branch.
rationale: Discovery §2/§3: these three files are the parity-critical surface and the 17-step filter ordering is explicitly called out as needing extra care; coverage must prove every branch is exercised.

text: Add a benchmark regression test that runs a representative scan-heavy backtest, asserts it completes well under the 120s cap, asserts a warm rerun is near-instant (no exchange/DB kline work), and fails if runtime regresses beyond a threshold; mark it 'slow' and make it deterministic.
rationale: Discovery §4: _TIMEOUT_SECONDS=120 is both the symptom and the constraint; a perf guard prevents a later phase from silently reintroducing the O(scans×N_total) setup.

text: Add a contract test asserting metrics['total_trades'] is always present and non-null across ALL fixtures, including the zero-trade run.
rationale: Discovery §5: if total_trades is absent/renamed the UI routes completed runs to the 'no trades simulated' fallback (BacktestResultsPage.tsx:255) — looks like data loss.

text: Add a result-schema test asserting BacktestResults/BacktestMetrics keys remain a superset of the current ~45 keys (additive optional-nullable only; no rename/retype/remove) and that the equity-curve manifest hashes the FULL stored JSONB, not the LTTB-downsampled GET view.
rationale: Discovery §5: the frontend depends on the exact key set, and equity_curve is LTTB-downsampled on GET — hashing the API view instead of the stored JSONB would make the parity manifest meaningless.

text: Add a NUMERIC finite-guard test asserting that an Inf/NaN in any metric or trade field is coerced/guarded (via _num at backtest_service.py:1449) so _persist_results does not abort the whole save.
rationale: Discovery §5: a single Inf/NaN aborts persistence; float64/numba paths newly introduce float specials the pure-Decimal engine never produced.

text: Add a guard test asserting max_same_sector remains an intentional no-op in the engine AND the service still emits the max_same_sector_not_enforced warning.
rationale: Discovery §8: max_same_sector is deliberately unenforced (needs the IO sector service); a well-meaning refactor that 'fixes' it would change trade selection and break parity.

text: Add a determinism/repeatability test asserting identical config + identical inputs yield identical output across repeated runs, with no dict/set iteration-order leakage into trades or equity_curve.
rationale: open_symbols is derived from a set and the global timeline is sorted-unique; any reliance on hash ordering would make the bit-identical guard flaky and mask real regressions.

text: Add sweep-equivalence tests asserting parallel execution (asyncio.gather / ProcessPool) yields per-combo results identical to serial, that the initializer-shipped snapshot yields identical results to per-combo arg passing, and that per-combo write_result ON CONFLICT(sweep_id, config_hash) is crash-resume idempotent.
rationale: Findings RC-13/RC-14/RC-15: parallelization, one-time snapshot shipping, and rank stamping must not alter per-combo outputs and must survive partial-completion reruns without duplication.

text: Add a cooperative-cancel test asserting a mid-run cancel (threading.Event checked ~every 100 candles) still halts promptly and leaves a clean run state in the optimized engine.
rationale: Discovery §4: cancel is a lifecycle contract; the SoA/numba inner loop must keep honoring the cancel check it currently performs per 100 candles.

text: [PERF] The on_progress callback + throttled DB progress_pct writes MUST NOT dominate a sub-10s run: progress emission MUST be time/percentage-throttled (not per-candle) so total progress DB writes stay O(100) regardless of candle count, and a test MUST assert progress overhead is < 2% of engine wall-time on the canonical fixture.
rationale: At 5M candle-evals/sec the per-100-candle progress hook fires tens of thousands of times/sec; an un-throttled callback/DB write becomes the new dominant cost. No existing item budgets progress overhead against the now-fast engine.

text: [ENGINE] Because the @njit nopython kernel cannot invoke the Python progress/cancel callbacks, the kernel MUST run in bounded candle-chunks with the threading.Event cancel check and progress emission performed BETWEEN chunks; chunk size MUST keep JIT throughput at the Phase-4 target while still observing a cancel within a bounded wall-time (e.g. ≤ 250 ms), verified by a test.
rationale: #208 asserts cancel correctness and #15 asserts kernel throughput, but neither addresses that a nopython kernel cannot call back into Python — forcing external chunking whose granularity trades off throughput vs cancel latency.

text: [PERF] Total worker/thread parallelism MUST be globally bounded so that _MAX_CONCURRENT runs each launching a sweep cannot oversubscribe cores (Σ active workers ≤ a configured CPU budget); a test with 3 concurrent sweeping runs MUST assert the budget holds and no run's latency degrades > 2× versus solo execution.
rationale: #26 raises per-sweep concurrency toward cpu_count and #103 covers cache correctness, but nothing bounds aggregate parallelism when concurrent runs each fan out — the classic 3×cpu_count oversubscription that thrashes and blows latency budgets.

text: [DEP] NUMBA_NUM_THREADS and the numpy BLAS/OpenMP pools (OMP_NUM_THREADS/OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS) MUST be explicitly pinned and coordinated with sweep concurrency so nested parallelism (asyncio.gather × numba threads × BLAS) never spawns > cpu_count active threads; a test MUST assert the configured caps are applied at import.
rationale: Three independent thread sources (asyncio-driven engine threads, numba internal threads, BLAS) compound into thread explosion on multi-core hosts; no existing item pins or coordinates them.

text: [TEST] The benchmark regression gate MUST be flake-resistant on shared CI: report median-of-N (N≥3) after discarding a warmup iteration, use CPU-time and/or candle-eval count as the PRIMARY signal with wall-time secondary, and combine an absolute floor with the relative-delta threshold so hardware noise alone cannot fail the gate.
rationale: #36 defines a >5% wall-time regression gate but a raw single-shot wall-time check on noisy CI hardware is flaky; the methodology to make the gate stable is unspecified.

text: [PERF] The post-deploy worst case — first run with cold JIT compile, empty in-process LRU/hot cache, and cold columnar files — MUST be measured and MUST complete within the 120s cap; a cold-start test MUST assert boot-time JIT warm + first cold run ≤ cap, proving warm-at-boot is actually wired (not assumed).
rationale: #33/#143 exclude compile by 'warming once,' but no item asserts the genuine cold-start end-to-end (cold JIT + cold caches + cold Parquet, the true post-deploy first run) actually fits the cap.

text: [PERF] An ABSOLUTE sweep latency budget MUST be defined and gated on the canonical fixture (e.g. a 100-combo warm sweep at default concurrency < 60s, a 500-combo sweep < 5 min), distinct from the relative speedup ratio of requirement #29.
rationale: #29 only asserts a speedup ratio vs serial baseline; a slow-but-proportionally-faster sweep still passes #29 while violating any real operational throughput need. An absolute wall-time budget is missing.

text: [PERF] Peak process RSS for a K-combo sweep at concurrency C MUST stay ≤ base_snapshot + C×per-combo-working-set + bounded-hot-cache and MUST NOT grow with K; a sweep memory test MUST assert RSS is flat across combo count beyond the concurrency-bounded ceiling.
rationale: #30 bounds RSS for a single run only; a long sweep can leak/accumulate per-combo position books and grow the hot cache unbounded across combos — no sweep-level memory ceiling exists.

text: [CACHE] The in-process Arrow/SoA hot cache MUST be sized to retain the entire canonical fits-in-RAM working set so that WITHIN one sweep no symbol/range is re-loaded or re-converted after first touch; a test MUST assert per-combo kline-load count == 0 and SoA-conversion count == 0 for combos 2..K.
rationale: #32 covers warm-rerun-from-RAM but not intra-sweep no-thrash: an undersized cache could evict and reload the working set repeatedly across combos. The 'fits in RAM (~50-100MB)' sizing fact from findings §4.1 makes a no-eviction guarantee achievable and testable.

text: [ENGINE] The raw-kline→structure-of-arrays conversion MUST be memoized per (symbol, interval, range) so the dict→int64/float64 array build is paid once and reused across combos and reruns; a test MUST assert the conversion routine is invoked once per symbol-range across a K-combo sweep, not K times.
rationale: #31 mandates materializing columnar arrays but not caching the conversion; findings §4.3 'convert-once: cache the converted numpy SoA arrays' is unrepresented, so the parse cost would be re-paid every combo.

text: [PERF] Sweep benchmarking MUST separate first-combo COLD cost (load + SoA-convert + JIT warm) from STEADY-STATE per-combo cost, and MUST assert steady-state per-combo time approaches the engine-only floor (within ~1.5×), proving amortized throughput is compute-bound not setup-bound.
rationale: #29 measures aggregate speedup, masking whether per-combo cost is dominated by one-time cold setup. A steady-state floor is the real per-combo throughput metric and is unspecified.
